title: Surprise Anime Screening at GCFF in Australia
display_title: Surprise Anime Screening at GCFF in Australia
wikitext:
[]: "Surprise Anime Screening" at GCFF in Australia             Quote Post by beecass » Thu Mar 28, 2013 8:26 am Not sure if this is the right place to post this, but will move if necessary. The Gold Coast Film Festival happens vaguely "annually" if the organisers feel like it or have enough money or interest to put it on in any particular year, but it's happening again in 2013 from the 18th to the 28th of April. The festival has a fairly good reputation as a place to see lot of alternative and foreign cinema in what amounts to a cultural wasteland, and has included a few anime screenings and activities the last few years it's been on. Madman is the Australian distributor for Evangelion, and is pretty great at looking after their audience, and have had a lot of great faces and screenings in the Gold Coast area - recently (within the last two years, I can't remember the specifics) they had Makoto Shinkai come along for a great Q&A session to go with the Australian premiere screening of 5 Centimetres per Second , and have been pretty solid about making sure that Australian audiences have access to anime films whilst audiences are still excited about them - this year at the GCFF, they're running Wolf Children and a Robotech doco. Madman's been pretty good at looking after Australian Eva fans - ReelAnime in 2010 screened 1.0 and 2.0 on consecutive days fairly soon after 2.0's release, and most Evangelion and anime fans in general in Australia are already aware of the Evangelion Art Exhibition Tour making its way to a number of cities in April-June, including the Gold Coast at the. On the GCFF website , their anime program mentions a "SURPRISE anime screening soon to be announced" alongside the films already screening. Giving the handy proximity of the 3.0 BD release, the Eva art tour, and Madman's reputation for bringing Eva and anime content in general to Australia shortly after release, my hunch and general word on the street is that the screening is going to be for 3.0 (3.33 if they can get an early press). I live on the Gold Coast and am often in touch with people involved in the GCFF due to study and work, but they're playing their cards pretty close to the chest about what the screening could be. My guess is that Madman and the festival organisers are unsure whether or not they will be able to screen the film, or are saving the announcement for Yuko Miyamura's visit to the Gold Coast leg of the art tour.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:00 pm That would be killer if it is Q, but would Madman, or any other distributors for that matter, have their hands on a "proper" set of subs yet? I can't imagine them using fansubs for a festival screening, regardless of the quality/accuracy of the translation... I'll reiterate though that I'm fucking stoked to get my hands on a 1080p BD rip next month (hopefully), but seeing this movie in a theatre simply blew me away. And I'm really hoping others can experience it in that same way.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:02 pm I have had this possibility in mind since the art tour was announced, but to be honest, I do feel that this is rather soon and that it is unlikely that Madman would go on saying nothing if that they had a) gotten the rights b) were confident enough about it to tell GCFF organisers. It is probably something else, maybe another showing of the Madoka films, the K-On Film (which IIRC showed with the Japanese film festival in Sydney late last year), the From Up on Poppy Hill dub, or possibly Grave of the Fireflies since IIRC that is having a theatrical run in North America soon to tie in with the blu-ray release. Or maybe Berserk, lol.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:14 pm Blue Monday wrote: I'll reiterate though that I'm fucking stoked to get my hands on a 1080p BD rip next month (hopefully), but seeing this movie in a theatre simply blew me away. And I'm really hoping others can experience it in that same way.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:17 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Same here, man. I'm going to try and make my room into as close an approximation of a movie theater as I can!

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:20 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Same here, man. I'm going to try and make my room into as close an approximation of a movie theater as I can!

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:22 pm Blue Monday wrote: Haha, Raff I'm so looking forward to your thoughts and reaction to this movie, man...

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:28 pm I am not spending a thousand dollars on airplane tickets just to see a movie. Good luck, my Aussie friends.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:32 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: 42" screen, BOSE surround sound, Japanese snacks. My body is ready.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Mar 29, 2013 8:24 am Blue Monday wrote: That would be killer if it is Q, but would Madman, or any other distributors for that matter, have their hands on a "proper" set of subs yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Fri Mar 29, 2013 11:48 pm Hmm, *In best Walter Sobchak voice* I did not know that... That's pretty cool actually.

[]:             Quote Post by beecass » Tue Apr 02, 2013 6:43 am The subs that were on 2.0 for 2010's screening at ReelAnime were done in-house, wouldn't be a stretch for them to do it again. That being said, looks like the surprise screening is the world premiere of Makoto Shinkai's new film , which I'm equally stoked for, I adored Five Centimetres per Second . Admittedly, it's still a bit soon to see Q here compared to the timing of other screenings, and I was kind of just hoping, but I've got no doubt we'll see it in a theatre in Australia in the first half of the year.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Apr 04, 2013 5:06 pm tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Thu Apr 04, 2013 5:45 pm Ouch, that's a real ball buster.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Thu Apr 04, 2013 7:57 pm HA HA CALLED IT /moves thread to offtopic/anime/

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Tue Apr 09, 2013 2:46 am "Good things come in threes." Anyone know the dig with Madman's three surprise announcements coming up at the Melbourne Supanova? I'm seeing a lot of comments for the third Madoka movie and Q...

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Tue Apr 09, 2013 3:41 am I believe the dig is that they intend to announce stuff at their madman panel at melbourne supernova this what they do this is their job but I must admit a surprise announcement at an industry panel at a popular pop culture festival seems a little small for 3.0 announcement

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Apr 09, 2013 4:16 am The third Madoka film isn't expected, even in Japan, until the autumn anyway, it is reported ; so it's a bit early for announcements elsewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Tue Apr 09, 2013 6:01 pm Blue Monday wrote: the dig

[]:             Quote Post by Comic.R » Wed Apr 10, 2013 10:40 am I'm pretty jealous Australia gets the special treatment! I understand why, I just wish I could go. Between work and how expensive plane tickets are it's just not possible. There's also that Evangelion Rebuild art exhibit happening on the Gold Coast, very lucky.
